title: taenitis blechnoides var. interrupta
intro: Taenitis blechnoides var. interrupta, also known as the Tahina or Interrupted Tahina, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In traditional medicine, the leaves of Taenitis blechnoides var. interrupta are typically prepared as a tea or infusion, with some communities also using it in a poultice or as a topical application.
